name: Old Fashioned

spec.method_detail: Add sugar, bitters, and water to rocks glass. Dissolve sugar. Add a large rock. Pour whiskey over. Stir gently. Express orange peel.

history: The 'Improved' cocktail craze of the 1870s added liqueurs and absinthe to the basic cocktail (spirit, sugar, water, bitters). Purists ordered their drinks 'the old-fashioned way' — thus the name. It was first explicitly named in print in the 1880s. The most important thing: don't muddle fruit in it. The cherry-and-orange Wisconsin Old Fashioned is a regional variant, not the classic.
